errors.laserOverheated: Oh no! Your laser canon overheated from shooting too fast! You need to move before you can shoot a second time.
errors.missedShot: Oh no, you missed. Wasting ammo is not allowed!
errors.moveOffEdge: Oh no, you tried to move off the edge!

checks.notShotAllAliens: You didn't shoot down all the aliens.
checks.functionNotDefined: You need to define a shootIfAlienAbove function.
checks.functionNotCalled: You've defined shootIfAlienAbove, but you need to actually call it in your solution.

battleProcedures.name: Create a shootIfAlienAbove function and use it to win the game
battleProcedures.description: Extract the shooting logic into a shootIfAlienAbove() function, then use it alongside the movement logic to defeat all aliens.

battleProcedures.name: Battle Procedures
battleProcedures.description: Defeat all aliens using your shootIfAlienAbove function

wherePutFunction.question: Where should I put the `shootIfAlienAbove` function?
wherePutFunction.answer: Define it at the top of your code, before the loop. Once it is defined you can call it from inside the loop as many times as you like.

whatGoesInside.question: What goes inside `shootIfAlienAbove`?
whatGoesInside.answer: Use an `if` statement that checks `isAlienAbove()`. When that returns `true`, call `shoot()`. That is the whole body of the function.

whatStaysInLoop.question: What stays in the loop?
whatStaysInLoop.answer: All the movement logic stays in the loop, the same as in the scroll-and-shoot exercise. Each iteration: call `shootIfAlienAbove()`, then move the cannon, then flip direction at the boundaries.

trackCannon.question: How do I keep track of where the cannon is and which way it is going?
trackCannon.answer: Use two variables that you update each iteration. For example, a `position` variable that goes up or down, and a `direction` variable that is `"right"` or `"left"` and flips when you hit an edge.

moveLeft.description: Moves the laser cannon **one position to the left**. If you try to move off the left edge of the screen, you'll lose the game!

moveRight.description: Moves the laser cannon **one position to the right**. If you try to move off the right edge of the screen, you'll lose the game!

shoot.description: Shoots the laser upwards. **Only shoot when there's an alien above you**, or you'll lose the game! The laser cannon overheats easily, so you must move before shooting again.

isAlienAbove.description: Returns `true` if there's an alien directly above the laser cannon, or `false` if not. Use this to check before shooting!
